title: Rows, cells and sections

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Rows, cells and sections; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Rows, cells and sections
sources.claim: Data tables use table, row groups, rows, header cells, and data cells to preserve two-dimensional relationships.

sources.locator: MDN HTML Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Rows, cells and sections
sources.claim: Data tables use table, row groups, rows, header cells, and data cells to preserve two-dimensional relationships.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Data tables use table, row groups, rows, header cells, and data cells to preserve two-dimensional relationships.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A score table places column headings in thead and one learner record in each tbody row. Your goal is to create a valid table structure from a small data matrix. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Heading hierarchy. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Data tables use table, row groups, rows, header cells, and data cells to preserve two-dimensional relationships. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Tables should not be used for page layout, and visual alignment alone does not expose row and column relationships. This draft therefore rejects: Div elements styled as a grid automatically expose the same relationships as a table.

includes: Use table, row, head, body, foot and cell elements for tabular data only.
Create a valid table structure from a small data matrix.
excludes: Tables should not be used for page layout, and visual alignment alone does not expose row and column relationships.

outcomes: Create a valid table structure from a small data matrix.

examples.title: Rows, cells and sections: concrete case

walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A score table places column headings in thead and one learner record in each tbody row.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use native table structure for tabular data so row and column relationships remain programmatically available.
examples.result: The result follows because Use native table structure for tabular data so row and column relationships remain programmatically available.
